tdee.title: Energy needs
tdee.alertMessage:
Daily energy needs are calculated using the Kasarskis formula, specifically developed for ALS patients.

The calculation takes into account your basal metabolic rate (BMR), activity level based on ALSFRS-6, and ALS-specific adjustments.

This estimate serves as guidance. Discuss your individual needs with your doctor or nutritionist.

healthInfo.headerText: This information ensures the app works as accurately as possible. These details are not shared with help cure ALS.
healthInfo.firstSymptomsSubtitle: When did you first notice symptoms?
healthInfo.alsCauseInfo: About 90–95% of all ALS cases are sporadic, meaning without known family history. In 5–10% of cases, a familial (genetic) form is present.
healthInfo.alsOnsetRegionSubtitle: Where did the first symptoms appear?
healthInfo.alsOnsetRegionInfo: The region of symptom onset is an important factor for the individual disease course. ALS most commonly begins in the extremities (spinal) or in the oral/pharyngeal area (bulbar).
healthInfo.alsMotorNeuronInfo: The upper motor neuron controls movements from the brain to the spinal cord – damage causes spasticity. The lower motor neuron transmits signals to muscles – its damage leads to muscle wasting. In classic ALS, both are affected.

studies.title: Studies
studies.alreadyEnrolled: You are already enrolled in all matching studies.
studies.aboutText: Clinical studies are important for developing new ALS treatments. Participation is voluntary and can be ended at any time. Talk to your doctor before applying for a study.
studies.infoPageText: Clinical studies help develop new treatments and improve understanding of ALS. Participation is voluntary. You can review the details and decide together with your care team whether a study is right for you.
studies.infoPageHint: You can open this information again anytime at the bottom of the studies list.
studies.adjustFilters: Adjust your filters or reset them.
studies.noClinicStudies: Your clinic has not selected any studies yet
studies.studyNotFoundText: This study could not be found.
studies.aiTranslationNotice: This content was automatically translated using AI and may contain errors. The English original is authoritative.

pinOrder.title: Edit List
pinOrder.dragHint: Hold and drag the metrics to adjust the order.

alsfrsCard.fillQuestionnaireHint: Fill out the ALSFRS-R questionnaire to capture your functional status.
alsfrsCard.detailsDisclaimer: The ALSFRS-R is a standardized assessment instrument. Results do not replace medical evaluation.

progressRate.title: Progression Rate
progressRate.subtitle: ALSFRS-R Progression Rate
progressRate.formula: Progression Rate = (48 − current score) ÷ months since diagnosis
progressRate.formulaExplanation: The progression rate indicates how many ALSFRS-R points were lost on average per month since diagnosis.
progressRate.slowProgressionDesc: The disease progresses slower than average.
progressRate.intermediateProgressionDesc: The disease progresses at an average rate.
progressRate.fastProgressionDesc: The disease progresses faster than average.
progressRate.reference: Kimura F, et al. Progression rate of ALSFRS-R at time of diagnosis predicts survival time in ALS. Neurology. 2006;66(2):265-267.
progressRate.disclaimer: The progression rate is a statistical average and cannot predict individual disease course. Always discuss your values with your care team.

privacyInfo.title: Your Data, Your Control
privacyInfo.intro: This app was designed to protect your privacy. Your health data belongs to you – and only you.
privacyInfo.encryptionDesc: Your health content is stored encrypted locally and sealed on your device before Vault Sync.
privacyInfo.noAccessDesc: We cannot read actual health values, medications, questionnaire answers or notes in plain text. For sync and security, the server does process technical metadata.
privacyInfo.controlDesc: You decide what you record, import, share, export or delete. Vault Sync is part of the secure account, device and sharing logic.
privacyInfo.sharingDesc: When you share data with doctors or caregivers, it only happens at your explicit request. You can revoke it at any time.
privacyInfo.orgInfo: This app is provided by help cure ALS e.V., a non-profit organization dedicated to researching and fighting ALS.

verification.selectClinicSubtitle: Choose the clinic that should confirm your diagnosis
verification.nextStepsBody: You will receive a 6-digit code. Show this code at your next clinic visit. The clinic confirms through their portal that you have ALS. After confirmation, you can share your data for research.
verification.showToClinic: Show this code to your clinic
verification.rejectedMessage: Verification was rejected. You can start a new verification with a different clinic.
verification.revokedMessage: Your verification has been revoked by the clinic. You can start a new verification.
verification.noProviders: No clinics or ALS outpatient centers are currently available for verification. We are working to add more institutions.
verification.missingClinicHint: Your clinic or ALS outpatient center is not listed? We are working to add more institutions.
verification.verifiedSubtitle: Your ALS diagnosis has been confirmed by your clinic.
